items.correct: Yes — plants respire all the time to release energy; they also photosynthesise when there is light.
items.misconception: Plants only photosynthesise and do not respire; respiration is for animals only.
items.random_wrong: Plants only respire at night and only photosynthesise during the day; the processes never overlap.
items.why: Because photosynthesis is taught as the plant equivalent of respiration, students think it replaces respiration in plants entirely.

items.q: Where do plants get most of their biomass from?
items.correct: From carbon dioxide in the air — the carbon in plant tissue comes mostly from CO2 fixed during photosynthesis.
items.misconception: Plants get most of their mass from the soil through their roots.
items.random_wrong: Plants gain most of their mass directly from sunlight, which has weight.
items.why: Roots in soil look like the source of food/mass, but actually most plant dry mass is carbon from atmospheric CO2.

items.q: What drives water up the xylem in a tall tree?
items.correct: Transpiration pull — evaporation from leaves creates tension that pulls water up the continuous water column.
items.misconception: Roots actively pump water up to the leaves like a heart.
items.random_wrong: Capillary action alone is enough to lift water to the top of any tree.
items.why: Students assume there must be an active 'pump' system like circulation in animals; they miss that the evaporation at the top does the work.
It's glucose and O₂. “CO₂ and H₂O” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is NOT a limiting factor for photosynthesis?
items.why: It's Oxygen concentration. “Light intensity” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If photosynthesis rate doubles when light doubles up to 50%, what is the rate at 25% light if it is 8 units at 50% light?
items.why: The real figure is 4 units. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
